scenario: A logistics provider uses the Camunda BPMN Framework collaboration style and models a process with exactly one message start event to connect a human pool to a technical pool that handles 7500 shipments per day. A developer must decide how to start instances of the technical pool from a client SDK in Camunda 8.8.
question: Which statement matches what the Using the Camunda BPMN framework section documents when there is exactly one message start event?

options.text: Exactly one message start event in the whole process definition can be treated as if it were a none start event when starting a process instance, so the client may start instances by BPMN process ID without publishing a message

Exactly one message start event in the whole process definition must always be triggered by publishing the matching named message and a correlation key, with process-ID-based start reserved for none start events only

Exactly one message start event in the whole process definition must be replaced by a none start event before deployment, with Camunda 8 rejecting any process whose single start event is a message start event

Exactly one message start event in the whole process definition must be paired with a non-interrupting timer start event to avoid blocking the engine when the upstream human flow has not yet emitted the message

a.text: Correct. The Using the Camunda BPMN framework section says that if there is exactly one message start event for the whole process definition, it can be treated as if it were a none start event when starting a process instance. So a client can use the BPMN process ID start instead of publishing a message.

b.text: Incorrect. The whole point of the section is that a single message start event can be treated like a none start event for starting an instance; publishing a message is not required in that case.

c.text: Incorrect. The page recommends keeping the message start event for collaboration clarity; Camunda 8 does not require replacing it with a none start event before deployment.

d.text: Incorrect. The section says nothing about pairing the message start event with a non-interrupting timer; it only describes the equivalence to a none start event for starting an instance.

explanation: When a process definition has exactly one message start event, the Camunda BPMN framework guidance says it can be treated as if it were a none start event when starting a process instance, so the technical pool can be started by BPMN process ID without publishing a message.
